input.headline: Korean coast guard arrests helmsman who was on his phone when ferry ran aground

raw.headline: Korean coast guard arrests helmsman who was on his phone when ferry ran aground

winner: The coast guard said the helmsman's social media scrolling was the reason for the wreck, but they’re still trying to decide if it was a navigation error or just a very literal "scroll" of the coastline.

candidates.text: The coast guard officer told the helmsman, "You should have been looking at the horizon instead of your phone." The helmsman replied, "I was trying to look at the horizon, but the phone kept telling me I'd arrived at my destination.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'arrived at my destination' as a phone notification, which is both specific and surprising."}

candidates.text: The Korean coast guard arrested the helmsman for texting "I'm sorry" in Morse code when the ferry hit a rock.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the Morse code message adds humor and specificity to the situation."}

candidates.text: I told my GPS we're taking a scenic route, but I think it meant the one that leads straight into a coast guard's custody for texting and driving.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks direct connection to the Korean coast guard or the ferry incident. It's a bit generic and doesn't fully leverage the specific situation described in the headline."}

candidates.text: The coast guard captain asked the helmsman why he hit the rocks while scrolling, and the man replied, "I wasn't ignoring the navigation, I was just trying to see if the land was 'likable' enough to follow.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the 'likable' twist, but it's a bit forced and lacks natural flow. The setup could be more surprising, and the pun feels a little generic."}

candidates.text: The captain was so distracted by his phone that he didn't notice the ferry running aground — until the coast guard arrested him for texting while floating.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise and uses a play on words with 'floating' instead of 'driving,' which adds a mild surprise. However, it's slightly generic and doesn't fully leverage the specificity of the situation or the humor potential of the arrest."}

candidates.text: The coast guard said the helmsman's social media scrolling was the reason for the wreck, but they’re still trying to decide if it was a navigation error or just a very literal "scroll" of the coastline.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'scroll' with a pun on both social media and coastline, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: The helmsman was arrested for texting while sailing — turns out, he had no idea the boat was going off the rails.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat funny with the pun on 'going off the rails,' but it's a bit generic and lacks the sharp surprise or specificity that would make it stand out in a humor competition."}

candidates.text: The helmsman smirked as he showed off his brand-new "I Survived the Ferry" keychain—then realized too late that the one he really needed was for his phone’s lock screen.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the situation, but the humor feels a bit forced and lacks natural flow. The keychain reference is clever, but the punchline could be tighter for maximum impact."}

candidates.text: Apparently, the captain's excuse that 'I was just checking my texts' is now a new defense in maritime law—turns out, 'swipe right for disaster' isn't just bad dating advice.
